topic.what: Extends CDPR quantum attack from ideal to module lattices over 2k-th cyclotomic rings using trace orthogonality and MILP-optimized sign selection
topic.how: Decomposes module into rank-1 submodules via K-linear Gram-Schmidt; applies CDPR independently to each; reformulates sign-selection as mixed-integer linear program achieving universal constant discrepancy δ*≈0.4407
topic.why_matters: Security analysis of NIST ML-KEM/ML-DSA standards; confirms exponential barrier exp(˜O(√n)) remains for module lattice attacks

characteristics.what_it_measures: Hermite factor achieved by module reduction algorithm
characteristics.vs_baseline: matches CDPR for d=1, generic BKZ is exp(˜O(dn))
characteristics.context: Same exponential factor as ideal case; independent of module rank d
Universal constant independent of k; proved for k≤10, conjectured for all k≥4
Independent of rank d; √C constant for MLWE-distributed bases
C=1.08 for ML-KEM-768 (k=9); decreases with n

characteristics.what_it_measures: Rounding error bound ρ for Gram-Schmidt coefficients
characteristics.context: Enables bounded-precision NTT implementation; single split prime p=12289 suffices for ML-KEM

characteristics.what_it_measures: L∞-covering radius of embedded ring σ(R)
characteristics.evidence: Proposition 4.1 (lower bound √n/2, upper bound O(√n log n))
characteristics.context: Tight bounds using trace orthogonality and Hoeffding's inequality

characteristics.what_itasures: Security margin against algebraic attacks on ML-KEM-768
characteristics.context: Conservative estimate; actual security higher due to smaller typical δLLL

characteristics.what_it_measures: Time to solve sign-selection MILP to optimality
characteristics.context: Solved once per cyclotomic ring; k=10 uses 255 binary variables
